event: no history of close contact with infected individuals
sought care in a local hospital
right abdominal tenderness without rebound or guarding
drainage catheter yield of 150 mL of fulvous fluid submitted to microbiology
abdominal and pelvic computed tomography scan performed
irregular slightly low-density lesion in the right posterior hepatic lobe 41 mm × 56 mm with gas density shadow
liquid density shadow around the lower margin of liver
round-like low-density lesion about 0.8 cm in the right lobe of the liver
appendix thickened to about 20 mm in diameter
structure of the ascending colon near the ileocecal region disorganised
multiple gas accumulation and dilation in the bowel
fulvous purulent exudate and necrotic tissue in extraperitoneal space and abdominal cavity
partial postnecrotic defect in the peritoneum
massive epiploon adhesion in the right upper abdomen
perforation under the right side of the liver 15 mm
perforation in the right ascending colon about 100 mm from the ileocecal region
partial resection of the ascending colon
transferred to the intensive care unit
blood samples taken for aerobic and anaerobic cultures
Gram stain revealed short Gram-positive bacillus without spores
Eggerthella lenta identified by MALDI-TOF MS
Escherichia coli isolated from drainage fluid
repeat CT revealed decreased size of hepatic abscess
further 7-day course of intravenous ertapenem and teicoplanin
six-week course of intravenous ertapenem completed
